title: Firrtl fix invalid parameter check in paramod
body:
Hi,

The current firrtl backend checks for the presence of the `hdlname` attribute in a `Cell` that represents a module instantiation. The backend uses this attribute to determine the name of the module that is to be instantiated.

```C
if (cell->type.begins_with("$paramod"))
{
  log_assert(cell->has_attribute(ID::hdlname));
  instanceOf = cell->get_string_attribute(ID::hdlname);
}
```

However, Yosys does not store such an attribute in a `Cell`, but rather in a `Module`. The code has an invalid assertion that requires this property (which cannot exist where it is queried) and the backend therefore crashes if run on a parameterized module (not a parameterized blackbox module though as those are handled elsewhere).

For example, running the following verilog circuit with a parameterized input register chain connected to a simple passthrough module causes an assertion to fail.

```verilog
module top
(
  input          clk,
  input  [63:0]  in_top,
  output [63:0]  out_top
);

  wire [63:0] pass_in_wire;

  parametric_regchain #(2, 64) input_regchain(clk, in_top, pass_in_wire);

  nonparametric_passthrough passthrough
  (
    .pass_in(pass_in_wire),
    .pass_out(out_top),
  );

endmodule

module nonparametric_passthrough
(
  input	 [63:0]	pass_in,
  output [63:0]	pass_out,
);

  assign pass_out = pass_in;

endmodule

module parametric_regchain
#(
  parameter NUM_STG = 8,
  parameter WIDTH = 32
)
(
  input              clk,
  input  [WIDTH-1:0] in,
  output [WIDTH-1:0] out
);

  reg     [WIDTH-1:0] tmp[0:NUM_STG];
  integer             i;

  always @(*)
    tmp[0] = in;

  always @(posedge clk)
    for (i = 0; i < NUM_STG; i = i + 1)
      tmp[i+1] <= tmp[i];

  assign out = tmp[NUM_STG];

endmodule
```

Input yosys script:

```
read_verilog example.v
hierarchy -check -top top
proc
opt_clean -purge
write_firrtl example.fir
```

Yosys output:

```
5.1. Executing PMUXTREE pass.
ERROR: Assert `cell->has_attribute(ID::hdlname)' failed in backends/firrtl/firrtl.cc:698.
```

This PR fixes the following issues:
- Remove erroneous check for the `hdlname` attribute and fetch the instantiated `Module`'s name from the `Module`'s definition.

- Remove some code duplication by regrouping handling of parametric and non-parametric modules in a single place.

- Remove emission of an inline comment in a cell declaration. There is a branch in the code where instances can be declared as follows:

      inst x /* some comment */ of module_x

  However, firrtl does not support such `/* ... */` comments and this looks like a previous verilog-like interpretation of what firrtl comments look like.

The generated output for the example above is a bit large due to the wiring generated by the backend, but I'll add it here to show that the pass produces valid firrtl:

```firrtl
circuit top:
  module _paramod_parametric_regchain_NUM_STG_2_WIDTH_64:
    input clk: UInt<1>
    input in: UInt<64>
    output out: UInt<64>

    wire _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_18: UInt<64>
    wire tmp_1_: UInt<64>
    reg _procdff_16: UInt<64>, asClock(clk)
    reg _procdff_17: UInt<64>, asClock(clk)
    wire _0: UInt<64>


    _procdff_16 <= in
    _procdff_17 <= tmp_1_
    _0 <= _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_18

    _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_18 <= bits(_procdff_17, 63, 0)
    out <= bits(_0, 63, 0)
    tmp_1_ <= bits(_procdff_16, 63, 0)

  module nonparametric_passthrough:
    input pass_in: UInt<64>
    output pass_out: UInt<64>

    wire _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_19: UInt<64>
    wire _1: UInt<64>
    wire _2: UInt<64>


    _1 <= pass_in
    _2 <= _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_19

    _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_19 <= bits(_1, 63, 0)
    pass_out <= bits(_2, 63, 0)

  module top:
    input clk: UInt<1>
    input in_top: UInt<64>
    output out_top: UInt<64>

    wire _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_20: UInt<64>
    wire pass_in_wire: UInt<64>
    wire _3: UInt<64>


    _3 <= _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_20

    inst input_regchain of _paramod_parametric_regchain_NUM_STG_2_WIDTH_64
    input_regchain.clk <= clk
    input_regchain.in <= in_top
    inst passthrough of nonparametric_passthrough
    passthrough.pass_in <= pass_in_wire
    _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_20 <= bits(passthrough.pass_out, 63, 0)
    out_top <= bits(_3, 63, 0)
    pass_in_wire <= bits(input_regchain.out, 63, 0)
```

ERROR: Assert `cell->has_attribute(ID::hdlname)' failed in backends/firrtl/firrtl.cc:698.

circuit top:
  module _paramod_parametric_regchain_NUM_STG_2_WIDTH_64:
    input clk: UInt<1>
    input in: UInt<64>
    output out: UInt<64>

    wire _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_18: UInt<64>

    reg _procdff_16: UInt<64>, asClock(clk)
    reg _procdff_17: UInt<64>, asClock(clk)
    wire _0: UInt<64>


    _procdff_16 <= in
    _procdff_17 <= tmp_1_
    _0 <= _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_18

    _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_18 <= bits(_procdff_17, 63, 0)
    out <= bits(_0, 63, 0)
    tmp_1_ <= bits(_procdff_16, 63, 0)

  module nonparametric_passthrough:
    input pass_in: UInt<64>
    output pass_out: UInt<64>

    wire _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_19: UInt<64>
    wire _1: UInt<64>
    wire _2: UInt<64>


    _1 <= pass_in
    _2 <= _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_19

    _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_19 <= bits(_1, 63, 0)
    pass_out <= bits(_2, 63, 0)

  module top:
    input clk: UInt<1>

    wire _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_20: UInt<64>
    wire pass_in_wire: UInt<64>

    _3 <= _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_20

    inst input_regchain of _paramod_parametric_regchain_NUM_STG_2_WIDTH_64
    input_regchain.clk <= clk
    input_regchain.in <= in_top
    inst passthrough of nonparametric_passthrough
    passthrough.pass_in <= pass_in_wire
    _auto_firrtl_cc_99_rename_invalid_rhs_references_20 <= bits(passthrough.pass_out, 63, 0)
    out_top <= bits(_3, 63, 0)
    pass_in_wire <= bits(input_regchain.out, 63, 0)
